page.title: Marbella vs Estepona Boat Rental: Which Marina Should You Choose?

data.meta_description: Marbella vs Estepona boat rental: compare Puerto Banús and Estepona marinas. Find the best base for your charter with our fleet of 18 boats. Start your trip.
data.summary: This page compares boat rental in Marbella and Estepona, helping readers choose the best marina for their charter. It covers distance, atmosphere, and fleet options from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Marbella departures from Puerto Banús; Estepona departures from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona.
Distance between marinas is 15 NM (28 km) by sea, about 30–40 minutes cruising.
Puerto Banús is busier, with luxury shops and nightlife; Estepona is quieter and more relaxed.
Our entire fleet of 18 boats departs exclusively from Puerto Banús.
Estepona offers calmer waters and closer access to Cabopino and Cala del Faro.
Marbella charter prices start at €749/2h for an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39.
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.

faq.q: Which marina is closer to the best beaches for swimming?
faq.a: Estepona is closer to Cabopino and Cala del Faro, both known for calm, clear waters. From Puerto Banús, you reach Cala Cortés in about 20 minutes. Both offer excellent swimming spots within a short cruise.

faq.q: Can I charter a boat from Estepona with your fleet?
faq.a: No, all our boats depart from Puerto Banús in Marbella. However, you can easily cruise to Estepona during your charter—it's only 15 NM away, taking 30–40 minutes depending on the boat's speed.

faq.q: Is it cheaper to rent a boat in Estepona than in Marbella?
faq.a: Prices vary by operator, but our Marbella fleet offers competitive rates starting at €749/2h. Estepona charters may be slightly lower for smaller boats, but you get fewer luxury options and no jet ski inclusions like our Mangusta 80.

faq.q: What is the nightlife like near each marina after a boat trip?
faq.a: Puerto Banús is famous for its vibrant nightlife, with clubs like Pangea and Ocean Club. Estepona is quieter, with relaxed beach bars and restaurants. Choose Marbella if you want to party; Estepona for a calm evening.

faq.q: Which marina has better facilities for boarding?
faq.a: Both marinas have modern pontoons and fuel docks. Puerto Banús is larger and busier, with more parking and crew assistance. Estepona is smaller but well-maintained, with easier access for private charters.

faq.q: Can I visit Estepona on a boat rental from Marbella?
faq.a: Absolutely. A day charter from Puerto Banús easily includes a stop in Estepona. With an 8-hour rental, you can cruise to Estepona, anchor for lunch, and return. Our Tier A boats cost €2,299/8h.

faq.q: What is the sea condition like around Estepona vs Marbella?
faq.a: Estepona tends to have slightly calmer waters as it's less exposed to easterly winds. Marbella's coast can have a light chop in the afternoon. Both are generally safe for all boat types in our fleet.

faq.q: Do you offer sunset charters from both marinas?
faq.a: We only operate from Puerto Banús, but sunset charters there are spectacular—views of La Concha mountain and the Golden Mile. Estepona sunset views are also beautiful but less iconic. Book a 2-hour sunset slot from €749.

data.body_html:
Deciding between a Marbella vs Estepona boat rental comes down to atmosphere, distance, and what you want from your day on the water. Both marinas are excellent, but they serve different crowds. Our entire fleet of 18 boats departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella, giving you direct access to the Costa del Sol's most glamorous coastline. Estepona, 15 NM west, offers a quieter alternative. This guide breaks down the key differences so you can choose the right base for your charter. For a full overview of our options, visit our [main boat rental page](/).

Distance and Travel Time Between Marinas

Puerto Banús and Puerto Deportivo de Estepona are 15 nautical miles apart (28 km). By sea, that's a 30–40 minute cruise at planning speed on a boat like the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax). A slower displacement vessel, such as the Lagoon 380 catamaran (11.6m, 10 pax), takes about 50 minutes. If you're based in Marbella, you can easily include Estepona as a stop on an 8-hour charter. From Estepona, reaching Marbella's Golden Mile beaches is equally straightforward.

Atmosphere: Luxury vs Relaxed

Puerto Banús is synonymous with luxury—superyachts, designer boutiques, and buzzing nightlife. It's the place to see and be seen. Estepona, by contrast, is a traditional Andalusian fishing port turned marina, with a laid-back vibe, tapas bars, and fewer crowds. If you want a party atmosphere after your cruise, choose Marbella. For a serene day on the water with a quiet dinner ashore, Estepona wins.

Our Fleet: All Depart from Puerto Banús

Every boat in our inventory—from the compact Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) to the majestic Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax)—departs from Puerto Banús. This means you get the full Marbella experience: leaving from the same marina as celebrities and billionaires. Below is a sample of our fleet:

BoatLengthMax PaxStarting Price
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2h
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2h
Mangusta 8024m12€4,719/4h (incl. jet ski)
Fairline Targa 12m12m10On request

For full details on all 18 boats, check our [fleet page](/fleet/).

Beaches and Swimming Spots Near Each Marina

From Puerto Banús, you're minutes from Cala del Faro, a rocky cove with clear water, or Cala Cortés, a sandy beach backed by cliffs. Head west, and Cabopino's dunes and nudist beach are a 15-minute cruise. From Estepona, the coast opens to longer stretches like Playa de la Rada and the quieter Playa del Cristo. Both areas offer excellent snorkelling and swimming, but Estepona's waters tend to be slightly calmer in the afternoon.

Cost Comparison: Marbella vs Estepona Boat Rental

Our Marbella charters start at €749 for 2 hours (Tier A: Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39), including skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is a standout at €4,719 for 4 hours, with a free jet ski. Estepona operators may offer smaller boats at lower rates, but you won't find the same luxury or inclusions. For a like-for-like comparison, Marbella offers better value for a premium experience. See our [pricing page](/pricing/) for more details.

Which Marina Should You Choose?

Choose Marbella (Puerto Banús) if you want:

  • Luxury atmosphere with nightlife options.
  • Direct access to our full fleet of 18 boats.
  • Proximity to Golden Mile restaurants and shops.
  • Jet ski inclusions on the Mangusta 80.

Choose Estepona if you prefer:

  • A quieter, more relaxed marina environment.
  • Calmer sea conditions for swimming.
  • Closer access to Cabopino and family-friendly beaches.

Remember, you can always cruise from Marbella to Estepona during your charter. Our 8-hour rentals give you plenty of time to explore both. For a quick sunset trip, the 2-hour option from Puerto Banús is unbeatable. Learn more on our [charter guide](/charter-guide/).

Frequently asked questions

Which marina is closer to the best beaches for swimming?

Estepona is closer to Cabopino and Cala del Faro, both known for calm, clear waters. From Puerto Banús, you reach Cala Cortés in about 20 minutes. Both offer excellent swimming spots within a short cruise.

Can I charter a boat from Estepona with your fleet?

No, all our boats depart from Puerto Banús in Marbella. However, you can easily cruise to Estepona during your charter—it's only 15 NM away, taking 30–40 minutes depending on the boat's speed.

Is it cheaper to rent a boat in Estepona than in Marbella?

Prices vary by operator, but our Marbella fleet offers competitive rates starting at €749/2h. Estepona charters may be slightly lower for smaller boats, but you get fewer luxury options and no jet ski inclusions like our Mangusta 80.

What is the nightlife like near each marina after a boat trip?

Puerto Banús is famous for its vibrant nightlife, with clubs like Pangea and Ocean Club. Estepona is quieter, with relaxed beach bars and restaurants. Choose Marbella if you want to party; Estepona for a calm evening.

Which marina has better facilities for boarding?

Both marinas have modern pontoons and fuel docks. Puerto Banús is larger and busier, with more parking and crew assistance. Estepona is smaller but well-maintained, with easier access for private charters.

Can I visit Estepona on a boat rental from Marbella?

Absolutely. A day charter from Puerto Banús easily includes a stop in Estepona. With an 8-hour rental, you can cruise to Estepona, anchor for lunch, and return. Our Tier A boats cost €2,299/8h.

What is the sea condition like around Estepona vs Marbella?

Estepona tends to have slightly calmer waters as it's less exposed to easterly winds. Marbella's coast can have a light chop in the afternoon. Both are generally safe for all boat types in our fleet.

Do you offer sunset charters from both marinas?

We only operate from Puerto Banús, but sunset charters there are spectacular—views of La Concha mountain and the Golden Mile. Estepona sunset views are also beautiful but less iconic. Book a 2-hour sunset slot from €749.
